D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Azo Coupling — Diazonium + Coupler → Vivid Colour

a diazonium salt Ar-N₂⁺ attacks an electron-rich aromatic at the para position to form the Ar-N=N-Ar' bridge — extending conjugation across both rings gives the deep colour of every azo dye

More than half of all synthetic dyes contain an azo (-N=N-) group. The synthesis is a one-step electrophilic aromatic substitution: the diazonium ion Ar-N₂⁺ (made by diazotising an aromatic amine with NaNO₂/HCl) is electrophilic enough to attack a phenol or aromatic amine at the para position. The new N=N bond extends the conjugated π system across both rings, dragging the absorption deep into the visible.

Toggle “before” vs “after coupling” — the two reactants on the left become the azo product, with measured λ_max and colour swatch. Cycle the famous example to compare methyl orange (basic conditions), congo red (cotton substantive), Sudan I (lipid-soluble).
